raw_response: A, A, A, A, A, A

responses.question: Confirm your frontend framework
responses.decision: Framework confirmed as React + Vite. All component architecture, state management, and build configuration will use React + Vite.

responses.question: Set your mastery threshold numbers
responses.decision: Mastery thresholds: Active→Near-Mastery = 2 consecutive correct; Near-Mastery→Mastered = 3 consecutive correct across 2+ distinct sessions with no reversals in the last 5 exposures. Maintenance review every 10 sessions.

responses.question: Choose your DeviceId durability strategy
responses.decision: DeviceId stored in both localStorage AND a 7-year SameSite=Strict cookie. On app load: if localStorage UUID is missing, read from cookie, re-seed localStorage, and restore Redis association.

responses.question: Choose your exam score estimation formula
responses.answer_label: Weighted by question count per topic
responses.decision: Estimated exam score = sum(topic_mastery_percent × topic_question_count) / total_questions. Topics with more questions carry proportionally more weight.

responses.question: Choose your infrastructure provisioning approach
responses.decision: Redis provisioned via Upstash MCP server (Claude Code calls MCP tools to create database and retrieve credentials). Vercel deployment and env var injection via Vercel CLI. Zero manual dashboard interaction.

responses.question: Choose your Maintenance state demotion rule
responses.answer_label: Strict: any non-fast-tap miss demotes to Active
responses.decision: Any deliberate wrong answer (response time ≥ 2s) on a Maintenance question immediately demotes it to Active state. Fast-tap misses (< 2s) in Maintenance are repeated within the current session but do not trigger demotion.
